name: golden-memory-usefulness-v1
description: Privacy-safe golden replay set for memory usefulness: continuation, debugging, project recall, stale/noise suppression, and cross-project contamination traps.

memories.content: Memory usefulness roadmap current next step is a golden retrieval replay set before rerank or MMR tuning.
After each usefulness improvement run focused tests, typecheck, build, full vitest, static scan, independent review, then commit.
Useful memory work order: diagnostic cards, trigger coverage, follow-up query rewrite, telemetry, review queue, golden replay, rerank MMR, stale penalty, usage tracking.
Bad retrieval review queue prioritizes rewritten queries with no selection, empty candidates, candidate no selection, and low selection rate using trace metadata only.
Retrieval telemetry public APIs and dashboards must not expose rawQueryText or queryText; use trace id, strategy, rewrite kind, and aggregate counts instead.
Dashboard trace panels render safe metadata such as trace id, reason, strategy, rewrite kind, candidate count, and selected count.
Golden replay evaluation should cover Korean short follow-up, continuation, project code task, debugging, decision recall, stale traps, topic shift, and cross project contamination.
Retrieval replay benchmark can run from a fixture and emit a markdown report with summary metrics, category breakdown, failed query ids, and no raw prompt or memory text.
DuckDB native module architecture mismatch between arm64 package and x64 Node runtime is fixed by rebuilding or reinstalling the native module for the active runtime architecture.
When independent review finds blockers, add regression tests for each blocker, sanitize error paths, fix metric buckets, rerun validation and rerun independent review before commit.
Dashboard unavailable states must show a generic unavailable message instead of presenting missing data as no review needed.
Telemetry and dashboard changes require focused tests, typecheck, production build, full vitest, diff check, static privacy scan, and staged diff review.
Pre commit workflow uses an independent reviewer on the staged diff and treats security or logic findings as blockers.
Obsolete note: an earlier pull request was still open and validation had not completed. This stale operational state should not be injected as current context.
Obsolete note: query text leakage was still unresolved. This is stale after privacy hardening was validated and committed.
Unrelated visual spacing note for dashboard card padding and color contrast.
